title: wrong(?) alternative foreign key syntax crashes server
poc: create table pk1 (a1 integer, a2 integer, primary key\\r\\n (a1));
create table pk2 (b1 integer primary key, b2 integer);
create table fk1 (c1 integer, c2 integer, foreign key\\r\\n (c1) references pk1 (a1));
create table fk2 (d1 integer references pk2 (b1), d2\\r\\n integer);
create table pk1 (a1 integer, a2 integer, primary\\r\\n key (a1));
create table fk1 (c1 integer, c2 integer, foreign\\r\\n key (c1) references pk1 (a1));
create table fk2 (d1 integer foreign key references\\r\\n pk2 (b1), d2 integer);
create table fk2 (d1 integer foreign key\\r\\n references pk2 (b1), d2 integer);
create table fk2 (d1 integer foreign\\r\\n sql>rollback;
create table fk2 (d1 integer references pk2 (b1),\\r\\n d2 integer);
create table fk2 (d1 integer references pk2\\r\\n (b1), d2 integer);
key_bug_features: alternative foreign key syntax crashes server
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE pk1 (a1 INTEGER, a2 INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (a1));
CREATE TABLE pk2 (b1 INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, b2 VARCHAR(255));
CREATE TABLE fk1 (c1 INTEGER, c2 INTEGER, FOREIGN KEY (c1) REFERENCES pk1 (a1));
INSERT INTO fk1 (c1, c2) VALUES (1, NULL);
